documentation.description:
EAstring - encoding-aware string - is a dynamic string type similar to Ada.Strings.Unbounded.Unbounded_String,
but it handles strings in all character encodings and frees you from character encoding troubles. It keeps track
of how each string is encoded and transcodes them automatically when necessary.

The child packages Latin_1 and UCS_2 contain subprograms for using EAstrings together with Ada's standard string
types. Latin_1 is for String and Unbounded_String, and UCS_2 is for Wide_String and Unbounded_Wide_String.

The child package IO provides encoding-aware input and output.

The child package OS and its descendants provide encoding-aware access to information from the environment.

The procedures and functions that manipulate EAstrings will transcode them as needed. The procedures may change
the encoding of their parameters when these are declared "in out". The functions will leave their parameters
unchanged but may return EAstrings in other encodings. There are also subprograms available that allow for
explicit transcoding (Transcode and Convert). In most cases it should not be necessary to use them, but if you
need to convert an EAstring to a byte sequence in a particular encoding you should call Transcode to make sure
that it is in the right encoding, and then retrieve the byte sequence with Bytes. You may also want to call
Transcode for performance reasons in certain cases. A series of function calls may lead to the same string
being transcoded over and over, and this can be prevented by explicitly transcoding it first.

The first character in an EAstring always has the index 1.

In this package, a character is a Unicode code point; not a byte, a code unit or a grapheme. In some encodings,
a code unit is one byte. In others it's two or four bytes. In some encodings, one character (code point) can be
encoded in several code units. A grapheme can be represented as one character or as a base character followed
by one or more combining characters. Most of the subprograms in this package count, compare and process code
points. A few deal with bytes.

Througout the EAstrings library, it is assumed that a byte is eight bits.

simple_types.name: Conversion_Stop_Cause
simple_types.code:
type Conversion_Stop_Cause is
   (All_Done,
    Target_Full,
    Incomplete,
    Inconvertible);

enumeration_literals.name: Inconvertible
enumeration_literals.description:
Reason why Convert returns.
  All_Done      : The whole input string was converted.
  Target_Full   : There isn't enough room in the target.
  Incomplete    : The source ends in an incomplete code sequence.
  Inconvertible : One of these errors happened:
                    1: The source isn't a valid encoded string for the
                       source encoding.
                    2: The source contains a character that can't be
                       represented in the target encoding.

array_types.name: Byte_Sequence
array_types.code:
type Byte_Sequence is
   array (Positive range <>)
   of aliased Interfaces.Unsigned_8 with
   Universal_Aliasing, Component_Size => 8;

array_types.name: EAstring_Array

documentation.description:
One of these errors happened during a conversion: 1: The byte sequence isn't a valid encoded string for its
associated
   encoding.
2: The string contains a character that can't be represented in the
   target encoding.
The string ends in an incomplete code sequence. Maybe it has been cut off in the wrong place.
An undocumented error happened in a C function.

An attempt was made to do an encoding conversion that the operating system doesn't support. Probably the target
encoding name wasn't recognized.

subprograms.code: function "&" (Left, Right : in EAstring) return EAstring

documentation.description: Concatenates two EAstrings.

subprograms.code:
function "*"
   (Left  : in Natural;
    Right : in EAstring)
    return EAstring
function "=" (Left, Right : in EAstring) return Boolean

documentation.description:
Returns True if and only if Left and Right contain identical sequences of characters (regardless of their
current encodings).

subprograms.name: Append
subprograms.code: procedure Append (Source : in out EAstring; New_Item : in EAstring)

documentation.description: Appends New_Item to the end of Source.

subprograms.name: Byte_Sequence_To_Fake_String
subprograms.code: function Byte_Sequence_To_Fake_String (Source : in Byte_Sequence) return String

documentation.description:
Unchecked conversion of Byte_Sequence to String. The result will not be a real String (unless the byte sequence
happens to be in Latin 1). For passing strings to functions that take String parameters but interpret them as
some other encoding.

subprograms.name: Bytes
subprograms.code: function Bytes (Source : in EAstring) return Byte_Sequence

documentation.description: Returns the byte sequence that an EAstring is encoded as in its current encoding.

subprograms.name: Close_Converter
subprograms.code: procedure Close_Converter (Item : in out Converter)

documentation.description: Frees a converter object allocated by Open_Converter.

subprograms.name: Convert

documentation.description:
State must be a converter object allocated by Open_Converter. Source must be a string encoded in the encoding
that this converter converts from. The string is converted and stored in Target. The indices in Source and in
Target of the last byte of the last character that was converted are stored in Source_Last and Target_Last,
respectively. The reason why Convert stopped is reported in Cause.

subprograms.name: Count
subprograms.code:
function Count
   (Source  : in EAstring;
    Pattern : in EAstring)
    return Natural

documentation.description:
Returns the maximum number of nonoverlapping slices of Source that match Pattern with respect to Mapping. If
Pattern is the null string then Pattern_Error is propagated.

parameters.name: Source
parameters.description:
Mapping : in Maps.Character_Mapping := Maps.Identity) to be added
later

parameters.name: Pattern
parameters.description:
Mapping : in Maps.Character_Mapping := Maps.Identity) to be added
later

subprograms.name: Delete

documentation.description: Returns Replace_Slice(Source, From, Through, Null_EAstring).

subprograms.name: Delete

documentation.description: Like above, but changes Source instead of returning a new string.

subprograms.name: Encoding
subprograms.code: function Encoding (Source : in EAstring) return Character_Encoding

documentation.description: Returns the encoding that an EAstring is currently encoded in.

subprograms.name: Fake_String_To_Byte_Sequence
subprograms.code: function Fake_String_To_Byte_Sequence (Source : in String) return Byte_Sequence

documentation.description:
Unchecked conversion of String to Byte_Sequence. For making EAstrings from objects that are declared as String
but contain some other encoding than Latin 1.

subprograms.name: Head
subprograms.code:
function Head
   (Source : in EAstring;
    Count  : in Natural)
    return EAstring

documentation.description:
Returns an EAstring of length Count. If Count <= Length(Source), the string comprises the first Count characters
of Source. Otherwise its contents are Source concatenated with Count - Length(Source) spaces.

parameters.name: Source
parameters.description: : in Unicode_Character := Space" to be added later.

parameters.name: Count
parameters.description: : in Unicode_Character := Space" to be added later.

subprograms.name: Head
subprograms.code: procedure Head (Source : in out EAstring; Count : in Natural)

documentation.description:
"Pad : in Unicode_Character := Space" to be added later. Like above, but changes Source instead of returning a
new string.

subprograms.name: Index

documentation.description:
Searches for a slice of Source, with the same length as Pattern, that matches Pattern with respect to Mapping.
The parameter Going indicates the direction of the lookup. Index returns the smallest (if Going = Forward) or
largest (if Going = Backward) index I such that the slice of Source starting at I matches Pattern. If there is
no such slice, then 0 is returned. If Pattern is the null string then Pattern_Error is propagated.

parameters.name: Source
parameters.description:
Mapping  : in Maps.Character_Mapping := Maps.Identity) to be added
later

parameters.name: Pattern
parameters.description:
Mapping  : in Maps.Character_Mapping := Maps.Identity) to be added
later

parameters.name: Going
parameters.description:
Mapping  : in Maps.Character_Mapping := Maps.Identity) to be added
later

subprograms.name: Index_Non_Blank
subprograms.code:
function Index_Non_Blank
   (Source : in EAstring;
    Going  : in Direction := Forward)
    return Natural

documentation.description:
Returns the index of the first (if Going = Forward) or last (if Going = Backward) character in Source that is
not a space (U+0020). If there is no such character, then 0 is returned.

subprograms.name: Insert

documentation.description:
Propagates Index_Error if Before > Length(Source)+1; otherwise returns Slice(Source, 1, Before-1) & New_Item &
Slice(Source, Before, Length(Source)).

subprograms.name: Insert

documentation.description: Like above, but changes Source instead of returning a new string.

subprograms.name: Length
subprograms.code: function Length (Source : EAstring) return Natural

documentation.description:
Returns the number of characters in the string. (This is not the same as the space it will take when printed,
even in a fixed-width font.) This may be a more expensive operation than one might think, because if the string
is in a variable-width encoding, a copy of it must be converted to a fixed-width encoding before the characters
can be counted.

subprograms.name: Open_Converter
subprograms.code: function Open_Converter (From, To : Character_Encoding) return Converter

documentation.description:
Returns a freshly allocated converter object that converts text from the encoding given in From to the one in
To. The converter must be freed with Close_Converter. Unsupported_Conversion is raised if the operating system
doesn't support this conversion.

subprograms.name: Overwrite

documentation.description:
Propagates Index_Error if Position > Length(Source)+1; otherwise returns the string obtained from Source by
consecutively replacing characters starting at Position with corresponding characters from New_Item. If the end
of Source is reached before the characters in New_Item are exhausted, the remaining characters from New_Item are
appended to the string.

subprograms.name: Overwrite

documentation.description: Like above, but changes Source instead of returning a new string.

subprograms.name: Replace_Slice

documentation.description:
If Low > Length(Source)+1, then Index_Error is propagated. Otherwise: � If High >= Low, then the returned
string comprises
  Slice(Source, 1, Low-1) & By & Slice(Source, High+1, Length(Source)).
� If High < Low, then the returned string is Insert(Source, Low, By).

subprograms.name: Replace_Slice

documentation.description: Like above, but changes Source instead of returning a new string.

subprograms.name: Reset_Converter
subprograms.code: procedure Reset_Converter (Item : in Converter)

documentation.description: Resets a converter object to its initial state.

subprograms.name: Size
subprograms.code: function Size (Source : EAstring) return Natural

documentation.description: Returns the number of bytes in the byte sequence that the string is currently encoded as.

subprograms.name: Slice

documentation.description:
Slice returns the characters Low through High in Source. It returns Null_EAstring if Low > High, and raises
Index_Error if Low <= High and High > Length(Source).

subprograms.name: Tail
subprograms.code:
function Tail
   (Source : in EAstring;
    Count  : in Natural)
    return EAstring

documentation.description:
Returns an EAstring of length Count. If Count <= Length(Source), the string comprises the last Count characters
of Source. Otherwise its contents are Count - Length(Source) spaces concatenated with Source.

parameters.name: Source
parameters.description: : in Unicode_Character := Space" to be added later.

parameters.name: Count
parameters.description: : in Unicode_Character := Space" to be added later.

subprograms.name: Tail
subprograms.code: procedure Tail (Source : in out EAstring; Count : in Natural)

documentation.description:
"Pad : in Unicode_Character := Space" to be added later. Like above, but changes Source instead of returning a
new string.

subprograms.name: To_EAstring
subprograms.code:
function To_EAstring
   (Source   : in Byte_Sequence;
    Encoding : in Character_Encoding)
    return EAstring

documentation.description:
Constructs an EAstring from a byte sequence and a character encoding. Source had better be a valid encoded text
in Encoding.

subprograms.name: To_EAstring
subprograms.code: function To_EAstring (Length : in Natural) return EAstring

documentation.description: Returns a string of Length spaces.

subprograms.name: Transcode
subprograms.code:
function Transcode
   (Source       : in EAstring;
    New_Encoding : in Character_Encoding)
    return EAstring

documentation.description: Returns a copy of Source encoded in New_Encoding.

subprograms.name: Transcode
subprograms.code:
function Transcode
   (Source       : in EAstring_Array;
    New_Encoding : in Character_Encoding)
    return EAstring_Array

documentation.description: Returns a copy of Source where each string is encoded in New_Encoding.

subprograms.name: Transcode
subprograms.code: procedure Transcode (Source : in out EAstring; New_Encoding : in Character_Encoding)

documentation.description: Makes sure that Source is encoded in New_Encoding.

subprograms.name: Transcode
subprograms.code: procedure Transcode (Source : in out EAstring_Array; New_Encoding : in Character_Encoding)

documentation.description: Makes sure that each string in Source is encoded in New_Encoding.

subprograms.name: Trim
subprograms.code:
function Trim
   (Source : in EAstring;
    Side   : in Trim_End)
    return EAstring

documentation.description:
Returns the EAstring obtained by removing from Source all leading (if Side = Left), trailing (if Side = Right),
or leading and trailing (if Side = Both) space characters (U+0020).

subprograms.name: Trim
subprograms.code: procedure Trim (Source : in out EAstring; Side : in Trim_End)

documentation.description: Like above, but changes Source instead of returning a new string.
